repository.name: Riemann-adelic
repository.description: This repository contains a fully unconditional and spectral proof of the Riemann Hypothesis using S-finite adelic systems, without invoking ζ(s) or the Euler product. The canonical operator D(s) ≡ Ξ(s) is constructed geometrically and validated via explicit formulas, Fredholm determinants, and Paley–Wiener uniqueness. Includes formalization in Lean

commit.message:
Migrate from per-map to per-texture .mat files

Each texture now has its own {texture_path}.mat file (e.g.
textures/common/quake/cop1_1.mat) rather than a shared per-map
maps/mapname.mat file. This mirrors how mesh models already handle
materials and enables far greater reuse across maps.

- Cm_LoadBspMaterials: loads textures/{name}.mat per texture
- R_LoadBspMaterials: drops map-level .mat preload
- R_LoadMaterial: tries textures/{name}.mat before suffix fallback
- R_SaveMaterials: writes per-texture .mat files for BSP models
- quemap AllocMaterial: seeds from per-texture .mat if present
- quemap LoadMaterials: iterates BSP materials, no per-map file
- quemap WriteMaterialsFile: non-destructive, per-texture output
- quemap MAT_Main: generates only missing per-texture .mat files
- quemap AddBspMaterials: bundles per-texture .mat and their assets
- qbsp/qlight: updated LoadMaterials() call sites

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(vscode): Phase 1 dashboard implementation — Preact + Tailwind + Zustand

Foundation:
- Preact 10 + preact/compat (3KB vs React's 42KB)
- Tailwind CSS with VS Code CSS variable mapping
- Zustand state management (5 stores: dashboard, system, agents, tasks, chat)
- Typed RPC message bus with correlation IDs over postMessage
- DashboardPanelManager with full bridge integration

Component Library (12 components):
- Button, Badge, Card, Input, Textarea, Select
- Tabs, Modal, Progress, Spinner, EmptyState, Tooltip

7 Dashboard Views:
- Overview: health cards, quick actions, system info
- Agents: search/filter, card grid, detail panel
- Tasks: task tree, submission modal, progress tracking
- Chat: markdown rendering, slash commands, code blocks
- Approvals: diff viewer, approve/reject, batch operations
- Memory: RAG search with type filters
- Settings: dynamic form generation with save/reset

Build: extension.js=34KB, webview.js=62KB (96KB total, well under 210KB target)

commit.message:
fix(spec-009-v2): address 8 critical/high bugs in subprocess isolation

Critical fixes:
1. create_engine() now guarded by try/except → prevents 120s hang on startup
2. put_nowait moved inside _spawn_lock → eliminates IDLE_EXIT TOCTOU race
3. Worker crash detection added → _fail_all_pending() prevents future hang
4. except Exception → except BaseException → CancelledError cleanup works
5. _result_reader_loop now exception-guarded → prevents task silent death

High priority:
6. release() failures wrapped with logging → no more deadlock on idle exit
7. Queue cleanup: old_q.close(); old_q.join_thread() → OS FD leak fixed
8. use_itn now reads from params, not hardcoded

Plus type safety improvements:
- create_engine() return type: Any → ASREngine (TYPE_CHECKING import)
- Queue type hints: Any → Queue[WorkerJob | None] / Queue[tuple[str, Any]]
- assert self._job_queue → if self._job_queue is None: raise RuntimeError

All 97 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: parse scientific notation amounts (e.g. 1e-8) correctly in json_to_satoshis

serde_json serializes small f64 values in scientific notation (e.g. 1e-8).
The old decimal-string parser failed silently on 'e', returning 0 satoshis,
which caused received transactions with small amounts to display as +0.00 TIME.

- Added parse_time_string_to_satoshis() with fast decimal path + f64 fallback
  for scientific notation
- Updated json_to_satoshis and json_to_satoshis_abs to use the new helper
- Added zero-amount filter in parse_transaction_list as defence-in-depth

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: pass CDN_BASE_URL to transcoder job on every deploy

The transcoder Cloud Run Job was only getting a new image on deploy,
but CDN_BASE_URL env var was never updated. This caused all videos
transcoded after the variable change to get stale cdn.ai-teammate.me
URLs written to the DB instead of the correct GCS public URL.

Fix: use --update-env-vars to sync CDN_BASE_URL from GitHub variable
on every deploy.

Also fixed in production:
- Cloud Run Job updated directly with correct CDN_BASE_URL
- 2 existing DB records (BB, El Dream) patched via SQL REPLACE

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: popover component added (#28)

This PR adds a first-class `Popover` component to the UI library so
consumers can use a consistent, styled popover API instead of composing
Base UI primitives ad hoc.

- **New component: `Popover`**
- Added `/src/components/popover.tsx` as a wrapper over
`@base-ui/react/popover`.
  - Exposes a library-consistent API:
- root state/control props (`open`, `defaultOpen`, `onOpenChange`, etc.)
    - trigger passthrough + `trigger` render prop
    - composition slots: `portalProps`, `positionerProps`, `popupProps`
  - Includes shared subcomponents:
    - `PopoverTitle`
    - `PopoverDescription`
    - `PopoverClose`

- **Public API export**
- Exported from `/src/index.ts` so it is available to package consumers.

- **Storybook coverage**
  - Added `/src/stories/popover.stories.tsx` with:
    - `Basic` story
    - `Modal` story
  - Demonstrates common usage and generated docs for the new component.

- **Example usage**
  ```tsx
  <Popover trigger={<Button variant="outline">Open Popover</Button>}>
    <PopoverTitle>Popover content</PopoverTitle>
    <PopoverDescription>
      This popover can contain contextual information or actions.
    </PopoverDescription>
    <PopoverClose render={<Button size="sm">Close</Button>} />
  </Popover>
  ```

commit.message:
Refactor/improve benchmark performance (#167)

* improve benchmark performance

Signed-off-by: kpango <kpango@vdaas.org>

* fix: nil pointer in StorePointer/SwapPointer; isolate go fix from format target (#168)

* perf(map): Add Map pointer methods to reduce allocations and latency
improved benchmark performance by optimizing pointer improved benchmark performance by optimizing pointer (#165)
Merge branch 'improve-gache-benchmark-performance-7845772806230586295' into refactor/improve-benchmark-performance

Signed-off-by: kpango <kpango@vdaas.org>

* Initial plan

* fix: handle nil pointer in StorePointer/SwapPointer and move go fix to separate Makefile target

- StorePointer and SwapPointer now treat nil value as Delete(key) to
  prevent phantom entries that are invisible to loads but counted by Len()
- Moved 'go fix ./...' from 'format' target to a dedicated 'fix' target
  to avoid version-dependent rewrites on every format invocation

commit.message:
feat: integrate /top5 priority aggregation into pm-architect (#2941)

* feat: integrate /top5 priority aggregation into pm-architect

Merge top5 priority aggregation directly into pm-architect rather than
as a standalone skill. Adds Pattern 5 (Quick Priority View) and Pattern 6
(Daily Standup) to the orchestrator.

Files added/modified:
- scripts/generate_top5.py: Aggregates priorities across backlog-curator,
  workstream-coordinator, roadmap-strategist, and work-delegator into a
  strict Top 5 ranked list (weights: 35/25/25/15)
- scripts/tests/test_generate_top5.py: 31 unit tests covering extraction,
  aggregation, ranking, tiebreaking, and edge cases
- scripts/tests/conftest.py: Added pm_dir, sample_backlog_items, and
  populated_pm fixtures for top5 testing
- SKILL.md: Added /top5 trigger, Pattern 5 and 6, updated scripts list

Closes milestone 1 of #2932

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: GitHub-native data sourcing for /top5

Rewrites generate_top5.py to query live GitHub data instead of reading
static .pm/ YAML files. Sources issues and PRs across multiple GitHub
accounts via gh CLI search API.

- Reads .pm/sources.yaml for account/repo configuration
- Fetches open issues and PRs via gh api search/issues
- Scores by: label priority, staleness, comment activity, draft status
- Falls back to .pm/backlog/ for local overrides when GitHub unavailable
- Restores original gh account after multi-account queries
- Weights: issues 40%, PRs 30%, roadmap alignment 20%, local 10%

Tested live: 116 candidates from rysweet + rysweet_microsoft accounts
29 unit tests passing (mocked gh calls + aggregation logic)

Implements Milestone 0 of #2932

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings in /top5 implementation

- Remove dead `env` variable and unused comment in run_gh()
- Simplify get_current_gh_account() from fragile stderr parsing to `gh api user --jq`
- Fix lstrip("-* ") bug in extract_roadmap_goals() using removeprefix()
- Rename shadow variable `l` to `lbl` in list comprehensions for readability
- Fix SKILL.md weight documentation to match actual code (40/30/20/10)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [skip ci] chore: Auto-bump patch version

* test: add outside-in agentic test scenarios for /top5 trigger

Five gadugi-agentic-test YAML scenarios covering:
- Smoke test: valid JSON output with expected keys
- GitHub source aggregation: end-to-end with real sources.yaml
- Error handling: malformed YAML, missing dirs, empty sources
- Local overrides: .pm/backlog + roadmap alignment scoring
- Ranking enforcement: top-5 limit, rank fields 1-5, score ordering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: enriched /top5 output with score breakdown, actions, near-misses, and repo summary

The previous output was a flat ranked list with no context for decision-making.
Now includes:

- Score breakdown per item (label_priority, staleness, activity components)
- Suggested action per item ("Merge, close, or rebase", "Fix immediately", etc.)
- Near-misses (items #6-#10 that just missed the cut)
- Per-repo summary (issue/PR counts, high-priority counts)
- Per-account summary (total work across repos)
- Full metadata preserved (labels, dates, days_stale, draft status, comments)

40 tests passing (up from 29), covering new features:
- suggest_action logic for all source types
- near_misses return from aggregate_and_rank
- build_repo_summary grouping and counting

Refs #2932

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: sync skill mirrors and remove ephemeral .pm/ state files

- Sync all new pm-architect files to amplifier-bundle/skills/ and docs/claude/skills/
  (fixes "Check skill/agent drift" CI failure)
- Remove .pm/ ephemeral state files (backlog, workstreams, delegations, config)
  that Repo Guardian correctly flagged as point-in-time state
- Add .pm/ to .gitignore to prevent future accidental commits

Generic session data API + roadmap as specialization

Generic layer:

- 'meta' is reserved (cannot be written via data API)
- GET /api/sessions/:id/data/:name — read any named document
- PUT /api/sessions/:id/data/:name — write any named document

Roadmap uses generic layer:
- getSessionRoadmap/setSessionRoadmap delegate to getSessionData/setSessionData
- PATCH /api/sessions/:id/roadmap kept for schema-aware merging
- Tools unchanged — still domain-specific for roadmap operations

feat(hm-client): add domain layer with repository, state machines, and ViewModels

- UiState sealed class (Idle, Loading, Success, Error)
- AuthRepository with in-memory cache and Room persistence
- PairingViewModel for OTP submission and token acquisition
- DashboardViewModel for daemon status and server probing
- CommandsViewModel for CRUD and synchronous command execution
- JobsViewModel for cron job management
- ServersViewModel for server registration and status probing
- ConsoleViewModel with WebSocket stream demultiplexing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(server): use Cloud SQL socket factory instead of direct IP connection

- Switch JDBC URL to postgres-socket-factory (already in build.gradle)
  which connects via Cloud SQL Admin API (HTTPS/443) — no Authorized
  Networks or Auth Proxy sidecar needed
- Remove DB_HOST / DB_PORT from workflow env-vars (no longer used)
- Set CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE=ai-native-478811:us-central1:learn-ai-db in workflow
- Add --add-cloudsql-instances flag to gcloud run deploy for IAM auth
- Reduce Hikari connection-timeout 20000ms → 5000ms (socket factory is fast;
  the 20s timeout was masking failures and causing 20-second response times)

Root cause: Cloud SQL had Authorized Networks=NONE, so direct TCP to
104.198.171.232:5432 was silently dropped → 20s HikariPool timeout on
every cold start and after connection resets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add .NET governance SDK for Microsoft Agent Framework integration (#109)

Implements the core agent governance primitives in C# targeting .NET 8.0,
enabling .NET enterprise teams to enforce governance on AI agents built
with the Microsoft Agent Framework.

Core components:
- PolicyEngine: YAML policy loading, rule evaluation, 4 conflict
  resolution strategies (deny_overrides, allow_overrides, priority_first_match,
  most_specific_wins) — matches Python PolicyEngine behavior
- AgentIdentity: did:mesh: format with HMAC-SHA256 signing (Ed25519
  migration path documented for .NET 9+)
- AuditEmitter: Thread-safe pub-sub event system with type-specific
  and wildcard subscriptions
- GovernanceMiddleware: MAF integration point for tool call governance
- GovernanceKernel: Facade wiring PolicyEngine + AuditEmitter + Middleware

Shared YAML policy schema works cross-language — same policies govern
both Python and .NET agents.

82 unit tests across 8 test suites, all passing.

Closes #80

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: agent-governance-toolkit
repository.description: AI Agent Governance Toolkit — Policy enforcement, zero-trust identity, execution sandboxing, and reliability engineering for autonomous AI agents. Covers 10/10 OWASP Agentic Top 10.

commit.message:
Deploy Storybook previews to Netlify from Buildkite (#349)

Build Storybook in Buildkite, deploy to Netlify via API, and post a
clickable PR comment link. Storybook previews are browsable from any
device (phone, tablet) without needing a local dev environment.

Architecture:
- Storybook builds in the existing Buildkite Docker step
- deploy-storybook.mjs uploads files to Netlify via file-digest API
  (uses only Node.js built-ins, no netlify-cli needed)
- Posts/updates a PR comment with the deploy URL via GitHub API
- Netlify site auto-builds disabled (stop_builds: true) to avoid
  Next.js Runtime v5 interference with the shared netlify.toml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Advance semantic analysis (src/semantic) (#7621)

* Agent: Advance semantic analysis (src/semantic)

* Initial plan

* feat(semantic): add sortPathsForReanalysis to ScopeTracker

Implements the missing step 2 of the documented hot-reload workflow.
Topologically sorts a set of file paths by file-level dependency order
using Kahn's algorithm, so each file's dependency declarations are
always registered before the dependent files are re-analysed.

- Add ScopeTracker.sortPathsForReanalysis(paths) with TSDoc
- Add test/scope-tracker-sort-paths.test.ts (15 focused tests)
- Update README.md with hot-reload workflow section documenting
  getImpactedFilePaths, sortPathsForReanalysis, clearScopesForPath,
  and a full end-to-end example

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(semantic): extract private helpers to reduce sortPathsForReanalysis cognitive complexity

Split the monolithic sortPathsForReanalysis implementation into five
focused private methods to keep each function within the sonarjs
cognitive-complexity limit:

- collectNormalisedInputPaths: deduplicates and normalises raw paths
- recordCrossPathDependencyEdge: adds a single directed dependency edge
- populatePathEdgesFromScopes: scans all scopes for one path
- buildPathLevelDependencyGraph: initialises and populates the full graph
- advanceTopologicalWave: advances the Kahn BFS queue by one wave
- topologicallySortPaths: runs Kahn's algorithm and appends cycle nodes

Public method sortPathsForReanalysis is now a 3-line orchestration call.
No behaviour change; all 14 sort-paths tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add team goal labels on rink during gameplay

Small color-coded team name labels appear at the bottom corners
of the rink during active periods, showing which team's goal is
on which side. Labels swap automatically when teams switch ends
between periods. Uses HTML overlay instead of SVG text to avoid
clipping by the rink's rounded corner clipPath.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tui: replace single-line inline editor with multi-line TextArea popup

The old editor squashed all block content into one line — Enter committed,
newlines were impossible, and code blocks were completely unusable.

Changes:
- ui/textarea.rs: new first-party TextArea widget (no external dep)
  · Lines-based storage, cursor-aware rendering with inverted-video cursor
  · Arrow keys, Home/End, Ctrl+A/E/K, Backspace/Delete, Enter (multiline)
  · render_textarea_popup() clears background and draws a styled popup
- app.rs: replace editor_text_input: Option<String> with
  editor_textarea: Option<TextArea> + multiline/label fields
- editor_commands.rs: start_inline_edit takes multiline+label params;
  handle_inline_edit_key routes key events to TextArea; commit joins lines
- editor.rs: textarea rendered as centred popup (90%×70%) overlay instead
  of the one-line bottom strip; EditorViewState carries editing_textarea ref
- Blocks that benefit from multiline editing: Code, Text, SpeakerNotes,
  List (all items shown one per line; split back on commit)
- List update_block_from_inline_text now splits on newlines to preserve/
  update all items, not just the first
- Tests updated: editor_text_input refs → editor_textarea; first_line()
  accessor used for single-line metadata assertions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve PR #45 review comments (P1+P1+P2) (#46)

- SSH_OPTS: use bash array instead of quoted string for proper word splitting
- install.sh: pull latest v11 code before requiring migration scripts
- execute-plan-engine: pass --model and value as separate args to delegate.sh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: tree view refresh performance - async discovery + debounce (#513)

* fix: tree view refresh performance - async discovery + debounce

Phase 1: Eliminate double-fire bugs
- Remove redundant treeProvider.refresh() from squad watcher callback
- Remove redundant treeProvider.refresh() from team.md watcher

Phase 2: Add tree-level event debounce (100ms coalescing)
- Route stateManager/terminalManager/labelManager events through _scheduleFire()

Phase 3: Convert discovery pipeline to async
- Add discoverAllAsync() + enrichWithWorktreesAsync() (fs.promises, execFile)
- Add scanSquadAsync() with parallel roster/charter/log scanning
- AgentStateManager.invalidate() now triggers async re-scans
- refreshDiscovery() delegates to refreshDiscoveryAsync()
- Sync versions kept for initial discovery at activation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR #513 review findings — bugs, DRY, tests

Bug fixes:
- Fix unhandled promise rejection in invalidate()/invalidateAll() with .catch()
- Delete dead sync refreshDiscovery() function (never called)
- Replace refreshInFlight boolean guard with pendingRefresh pattern
  (events during in-flight refresh are queued, not dropped)
- Fix _pending guard in AgentStateManager with _pendingRescan Set
  (concurrent invalidate for same squad queues re-scan)
- Add .catch() to all fire-and-forget refreshDiscoveryAsync calls

DRY refactors (~200 lines saved):
- Extract parseRosterContent()/parseCharterContent() in scanner.ts
- Extract pushParsedAgent() in agent-discovery.ts
- Extract processWorktreeResults() in unified-discovery.ts
- Document remaining sync calls in discoverAllAsync

Behavior change:
- _scheduleFire changed from leading-edge throttle to trailing-edge
  debounce (reset timer on each call) — ensures fire sees latest state

Tests added:
- Coalescing: 5 rapid events → exactly 1 tree rebuild
- Boundary timing: 99ms not fired, 100ms fired
- refreshInFlight guard: concurrent calls coalesced, error recovery
- _pending guard: rapid double invalidate triggers re-scan
- Error recovery: scanSquadAsync rejection clears pending state

Cleanup:
- Restore toHaveBeenCalledOnce() where coalescing is the point

All 1280 tests pass (39 files).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
